page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-8 is a general-purpose server instance belonging to the n2 family. It features 8 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The instance is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor operating at 2.8 GHz, configured with 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core under a dedicated allocation model. Cache specifications include 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 33 MB of L3 cache. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark results place the n2-standard-8 in the average tier for most operations. It achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1404.09 and a multi-core score of 7398.32. Memory bandwidth is measured at 413.79 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 50.27 GB/sec for large-block reads and 37.18 GB/sec for writes. However, multi-threaded performance shows some limitations, with weak results in Geekbench multi-core tests and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression.

The hardware profile and benchmark data suggest this instance is suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 6.9 GB/sec, and database operations, scoring 2838.94 in Passmark database tests. It also handles Redis SET operations at 7.12 million operations per second. Given its dedicated CPU allocation and average performance profile, it offers standard resource density for enterprise applications that do not require specialized GPU acceleration or local NVMe storage.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz. Configured with 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core, this instance provides dedicated CPU allocation but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates average performance across most metrics, including a stress-ng single-core score of 1404.09 and a multi-core score of 7398.32. However, it exhibits weaker multi-threaded performance, as seen in its Geekbench multi-core score of 4464.0. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the instance is suited for workloads such as static web serving, database operations, and Redis caching, offering a standard resource density for general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and dedicated CPU allocation, delivering average performance for web serving and databases.

tagline: An x86_64 general-purpose server with dedicated Intel Xeon processors and a balanced vCPU-to-memory ratio for standard enterprise workloads.

Dedicated CPU allocation with 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core
Average performance in single-core stress-ng and static web serving
Weaker multi-threaded performance in Geekbench and bzip3 decompression
Suited for static web serving, Redis caching, and database operations
